rulings.Case Title: HARTFORD ACCIDENT & INDEMNITY COMPANY VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Motion To Consolidate Actions 453038 And 462874
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 26, 2008, line 14, Defendants' Motion To Consolidate Actions 453038 And 462874. THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE COURT OF APPEALS HAS MADE CLEAR THAT HARTFORD "CAN RECOVER AGAINST THE SETTLEMENT AND IS NOT REQUIRED TO PURSUE A DIRECT ACTION AGAINST A THIRD PARTY" (HARTFORD I AT 7). THAT IS WHAT HARTFORD II IS ABOUT AND THERE IS NO REASON TO DELAY THAT TRIAL. ALTHOUGH BOTH CASES INVOLVE THE SAME UNDERLYING EVENT, IT SEEMS CLEAR THAT THE EVIDENCE TO BE OFFERED BY HARTFORD IN HARTFORD II IS LIMITED AS CONTRASTED WITH THE NEED TO ESTABLISH PG&E'S NEGLIGENCE IN HARTFORD I. MOREOVER, THERE IS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE RESULT IN HARTFORD II WILL MOOT THE NEED TO TRY HARTFORD I. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BONHACH VS. MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC A DELAWARE PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Leave To File First Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 26, 2008, line 3, CROSS-COMPLAINANT ALEXANDER LEFF, MALIBU PIER PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint. HEARING REQUIRED. IT APPEARS THAT THE MOTION TO AMEND SHOULD BE GRANTED BECAUSE THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT RELATES TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE PARTIES' DISPUTE AND THE MOVING PARTY HAS EXPLAINED THE BASIS FOR RAISNG THE CLAIMS AT THIS TIME. THE HEARING IS TO ADDRESS WHAT CONDITIONS, IF ANY, SHOULD BE IMPOSED RELATED TO THE JANUARY TRIAL DATE. =(302/PJM/JW)
